narrative: OMX30 opens Tuesday with structural bullish backdrop (+8.6% above 200-MA) but tactical headwinds that warrant caution. MACD has triggered a bearish crossover (hist=-1.727) contradicting the bull regime, while US 10Y yields surged +60bps to 4.336% creating DCF compression risk for tech/growth and refinancing pressure on real estate. ADX at 18.1 sits below our 20-point confidence threshold, reducing technical signal strength by 30%. The weighted correlation signal of -0.191% reflects mild risk-off sentiment despite yesterday's mixed closes (DAX -0.29%, CAC -0.11%, S&P +0.12%). Brent crude collapsing -3.16% to 104.8 eases input cost inflation, a modest positive for consumer names, but USD strength (+0.12% in USD/SEK) is the cleanest tailwind for OMX exporters.
morningBrief: OMX30 likely opens marginally positive on USD tailwinds and technical bull market setup, but expect choppy tape. ADX at 18.1 and MACD bearish crossover signal low conviction; use any pop toward 3180 (50-MA/resistance) as a sell-the-rally opportunity. Key watch: 3085 support (lower Bollinger band) holds or we test 3050. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) should see modest bid from 4.34% US yields and wide NIM outlook, but real estate (Balder, Castellum) faces headwind from refinancing math. Exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson) benefit from USD/SEK at 9.252 and weak EUR/USD, but NASDAQ sentiment overnight (-0.20% NASDAQ signal) caps upside. Commodities-dependent Boliden holds on copper +0.64% but gold weakness (-0.70%) is a drag. Watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—a strong print could spike yields further and trigger rotation into defensives.
drivers: Conflicting signals: bull market structure vs MACD bearish crossover and rising yields
USD/SEK strength supports exporters but offset by oil weakness and yield headwinds
ADX at 18.1 (below 20 threshold) reduces technical conviction; RSI 47.26 neutral with no mean reversion extremes
Global correlation weighted -0.191% signals modest risk-off tilt despite OMX resilience

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +60bps to 4.336% widens net interest margin expectations. Swedish mortgage/deposit spreads benefit from higher carry and refinancing dynamics.
Yield surge (+60bps US 10Y) directly pressures WACC and refinancing costs for property companies. OMX real estate valuations face DCF compression.
Yields +60bps compress DCF multiples. NASDAQ overnight no catalyst. MACD bearish crossover adds technical headwind to growth-sensitive names.
USD/SEK +0.12% to 9.252 supports export pricing. Brent -3.16% eases input-cost inflation. Balance sheet-heavy names benefit from industrial cycle resilience.
Copper +0.64% is a tailwind for Boliden, but gold -0.70% is offsetting. Oil -3.16% helps SSAB on energy/input costs. Net impact marginal.
Brent -3.16% eases fuel/logistics cost pressure; modest positive. USD/SEK strength marginally negative for H&M sourcing (weak EUR/SEK context). No strong catalyst.

watchList.reason: Copper +0.64% is positive but gold -0.70% is offsetting. ADX 18.1 (below threshold) reduces conviction. Wait for clarity on commodity direction.
Banks benefit from 60bps yield rise. NIM expansion outlook supportive. 4.336% US 10Y is attractive for capital allocation.
USD/SEK at 9.252 (+0.12%) supports export competitiveness. Industrial cycle tailwind from lower oil costs. Resistance at 52W high is -4.4%, room to run.
Yield +60bps directly compresses real estate valuations. Refinancing headwind likely. Defensive rotation into bonds may pressure REIT multiple.
watchList.catalyst: Swedish mortgage rate reset; OMRX spreads

watchList.reason: Tech headwind from yield compression; MACD bearish crossover adds sentiment risk. NASDAQ overnight weakness not supportive. Wait for reversal setup.
watchList.catalyst: Tech sentiment; MACD signal crossover above

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (consensus 217K, prior 220K). Strong beat could spike yields further and trigger tech/RE selloff. Swedish events: minimal.
topRisk: Further US yield surge (>4.40%) could trigger cascading DCF compression in tech/growth and accelerate real estate repricing lower. Trade war rhetoric escalation could spike VIX (currently 18.36) and reverse USD/SEK carry tailwind. MACD bearish crossover suggests mean reversion risk if ADX strengthens above 20.
technicalSummary: MACD bearish crossover (hist=-1.727) contradicts regime — RED FLAG
ADX 18.1 BELOW confidence threshold (20); reduce all signals by 30%
RSI 47.26 neutral, no extremes (no mean reversion risk)
Bollinger %B=0.51 in upper half but width 10.37% moderate — no breakout imminent
Stochastic K=21.7, D=35.7 — both undersold, slight upside bias but weak
ROC-90 +8.9% reflects strong 3-month momentum but 52W at 83% suggests maturity
VIX +1.89% to 18.36 — mild risk-off, not panic, but rising
